title: The Two Blind Men Beyond Jericho
subtitle: Two beggars at the roadside cry out, and the Light of light touches their eyes

stillness: Be still, for the Lord is passing by.
He who passes is the fountain of true light.
Like a rose, He is sensed before He is seen — wait for His fragrance.
gospel: After these things, as they went out from Jericho,
And behold, two blind men sitting by the wayside
heard that Jesus was passing by.
Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David.
And the multitude rebuked them, that they should hold their peace.
But they cried out the more, saying:
Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David.
And Jesus stood, and called them, and asked:
What will ye that I do unto you?
They say to Him: Lord, that our eyes may be opened.
And Jesus, having compassion on them, touched their eyes.
And immediately they saw, and followed Him.
behold: Behold the two blind men sitting by the wayside, that they might beg alms of those passing by — not far from the way of salvation.
They hear the noise of the running crowd, but see no faces; of their whole body they have nothing left but a voice.
And because they could not follow Him with their feet, they followed Him with their voice.
Behold them lifting their cry: Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David — confessing Him the Son of David in the flesh, and knowing Him to be God by inspiration.
Behold the crowd, looking on their filthy garments and not on the brightness of their conscience, rebuking them that they should be silent.
But behold — they cried out the more, pressing on, until they overcame the din of the crowd.
Behold Jesus standing still, waiting for them, for they could not follow; where would they go, who could not see?
And behold the fountain of true light: He touched their eyes, and at once they saw.
consider: O happy hearing, O light rumor! For many kings and prophets desired to hear, and heard not.
Such is the nature of faith: the more it is forbidden, the more it is kindled.
They were forbidden, and burned the more; first they cried because they were blind, then cried the more because they were forbidden to come to the light.
And Christ permitted them to be forbidden, that their desire might appear the greater.
Learn from this: that if we be cast down, yet draw near to God with earnestness, by our very selves we obtain what we ask.
There is a threefold crowd that hinders us: one from the flesh, one from the world, one from hell — that we be silent.
The crowd of fleshly desires rebukes by enticing; the crowd of the world's tyrants rebukes by persecuting; the crowd of hell's demons rebukes by deceiving.
When a man begins to live well and to despise the world, in his very newness he suffers cold and lukewarm Christians who reprove him.
But if he perseveres, those very ones who forbade him will obey him.
Cry out then; do not faint. He touched them as man in the flesh, but healed them as God.
And immediately they saw — for such enlightening was miraculous, not natural; for nature does not work in an instant.
prayer: Light of light, and fountain of brightness, Lord Jesus,
who, moved with mercy at the love of the two blind men crying out to Thee,
didst deign to touch their eyes and so enlighten them:
touch, I beseech Thee, my spiritual eyes,
and with the rays of Thy light illumine my conscience —
to contemplate Thee, to know myself, to instruct and help my neighbor.
Illumine my heart with the light of Thy divine grace,
that I may have Thee as my guide in all my ways, and follow Thee by imitating Thee;
and that I may, above all things, fear and love Thee who art above all,
and in all things do Thy will. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Sicut enim rosa antequam videatur sentitur, et antequam inveniatur tenetur in odoribus suis; sic et Domino transeunte, de longe duo cæci, antequam eum viderent, divinitatem ejus fragrantia senserunt.
latinJewel.english: For just as a rose is sensed before it is seen, and before it is found is held fast in its own fragrance; so too, as the Lord passed by, the two blind men from afar, before they saw Him, perceived His divinity by its fragrance.
